questions.q: Describe the characteristics of primary activities. How do they differ from secondary and tertiary activities?
questions.answer: Primary activities are the basic activities involved in the production of goods and services. They include agriculture, forestry, fishing, mining, and hunting. These activities are directly involved in the production of raw materials. Primary activities differ from secondary and tertiary activities in that they are the initial stage of production, whereas secondary activities involve the manufacturing of goods and tertiary activities involve the provision of services.
marking_scheme: Mentioning the characteristics of primary activities (1 mark)
Distinguishing between primary, secondary, and tertiary activities (2 marks)

questions.q: What are the main objectives of primary activities? Explain their importance in the economy.
questions.answer: The main objectives of primary activities are to produce raw materials and to generate employment opportunities. The importance of primary activities in the economy lies in the fact that they provide the basic inputs for the production of goods and services. They also contribute to the GDP of a country and provide employment opportunities to a large number of people.
marking_scheme: Identifying the main objectives of primary activities (1 mark)
Explaining the importance of primary activities in the economy (2 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the impact of primary activities on the environment. How can they be made more sustainable?
questions.answer: Primary activities have a significant impact on the environment, including deforestation, soil erosion, and water pollution. To make primary activities more sustainable, practices such as agroforestry, conservation agriculture, and sustainable fishing can be adopted. Additionally, the use of renewable energy sources and the implementation of eco-friendly technologies can help reduce the environmental impact of primary activities.
marking_scheme: Identifying the environmental impacts of primary activities (1 mark)
Discussing ways to make primary activities more sustainable (2 marks)

questions.q: A region has a total forest cover of 100,000 hectares. Of this, 50,000 hectares are used for fuelwood collection, and 20,000 hectares are used for timber production. Calculate the percentage of forest cover used for fuelwood collection.
questions.answer: Calculation: Total forest cover = 100,000 hectares. Forest cover used for fuelwood collection = 50,000 hectares. Percentage of forest cover used for fuelwood collection = (50,000 / 100,000) x 100% = 50%.
marking_scheme: Identifying the total forest cover (1 mark)
Calculating the percentage of forest cover used for fuelwood collection (3 marks)
Providing the answer in percentage form (1 mark)

questions.q: A farmer cultivates a wheat crop on a 50-hectare land. The yield of wheat is 2 tons per hectare. Calculate the total yield of wheat produced from the land.
questions.answer: Calculation: Total yield of wheat = Yield per hectare x Total land area. Yield per hectare = 2 tons. Total land area = 50 hectares. Total yield of wheat = 2 x 50 = 100 tons.
marking_scheme: Identifying the yield per hectare (1 mark)
Calculating the total yield of wheat (3 marks)
Providing the answer in tons (1 mark)

questions.q: A mining company extracts 150,000 tons of iron ore from a mine. The iron content in the ore is 60%. Calculate the total weight of iron extracted.
questions.answer: Calculation: Total iron extracted = Total ore extracted x Iron content. Total ore extracted = 150,000 tons. Iron content = 60%. Total iron extracted = 150,000 x 0.60 = 90,000 tons.
marking_scheme: Identifying the total ore extracted (1 mark)
Calculating the total weight of iron extracted (3 marks)
